text: Week three of the Relay. Dirtus is late. Very late. An hour past start time.
He finally texts: 'I found the location. It's perfect. Come see it tomorrow. Bring the poems.'

speaker: Grubwin
text: He found a location. He's been scouting a location.

speaker: Vermia
text: That's good! That means he's taking the Full Convergence seriously.

speaker: Grubwin
text: He's been taking it seriously since day one. That's what worries me.

speaker: Grubwin
text: I don't know. That's the other thing that worries me.

speaker: Mawla
text: Scatha doesn't manifest. We read some poems. We go home. Same as always.

speaker: Mawla
text: Then we deal with that when it happens.
Vermia has been very quiet. You look at her.

speaker: Vermia
text: I've wanted Scatha to be real for a very long time. Since the beginning. I built all of this because I wanted something — enormous — to be real.

speaker: Vermia
text: Now that it's actually happening — or getting close to happening — I'm scared of what I'll feel if nothing comes. And also scared of what I'll feel if something does.
This is the most honest thing she's said, maybe ever. Nobody fills the silence. You let her have it.
